subagent_reports.scope: 12-dimension acceptance review of complete improveSubAgentContracts diff: sub-agent schemas, report tool typing, packet specs/assets, prompts/templates, and verification fixtures

nonblocking_findings.label: praise
nonblocking_findings.what: Asset tests explicitly separate strict identity anchors from warn-first scope/done/stop/verification anchors.
nonblocking_findings.why: This directly protects AC3/AC6/C5 compatibility while making the new packet contract testable.

nonblocking_findings.label: praise
nonblocking_findings.what: Report tool schema now rejects string-serialized report payloads at the argument boundary.
nonblocking_findings.why: This is the key structural guard for AC8 and prevents MCP/schema drift from silently degrading typed report transport.

Targeted contract asset/schema/tool/message tests passed: 8 test files, 182 tests.

subagent_reports.scope: release readiness review for changed sub-agent contract files across 6 harden scanner dimensions

blocking_findings.label: blocker
blocking_findings.what: Worktree is not clean: docs/agent-tool-contracts.md has uncommitted release-relevant documentation changes after checkpoint 0ed132b.
blocking_findings.why: Release/archive readiness depends on a clean, intentional worktree. This doc change appears in-scope and useful, but it is not included in the checkpointed branch diff and must not be silently dropped or shipped as an untracked local state.
blocking_findings.fix: Main agent must either commit/include this documentation change or intentionally revert it, then confirm git status --porcelain is empty before release.

nonblocking_findings.label: praise
nonblocking_findings.what: Schema and packet-anchor changes are covered by focused asset/schema tests plus full-suite evidence.
nonblocking_findings.why: Existing evidence shows pnpm run check, pnpm exec vitest run --maxWorkers=4, and pnpm run build all passed; task verification also records targeted fixture remediation and rerun.

nonblocking_findings.label: praise
nonblocking_findings.what: Report tool now exposes a structured ScopedSubagentReportSchema argument rather than z.unknown().
nonblocking_findings.why: This structurally hardens object payload typing and directly supports AC8 without weakening INVALID_REPORT validation.

verification.evidence: No new code edits. Six scanners checked by diff/read/static evidence. Test coverage: task evidence cites pnpm run check exitCode 0, full pnpm exec vitest run --maxWorkers=4 exitCode 0 after fixture remediation, and pnpm run build exitCode 0. AI-slop/cleanup: git diff --check passed for committed branch diff and dirty doc diff; risky-token diff scans found no TODO/FIXME/HACK/debug/type-evasion additions in committed or dirty diff. Doc hygiene/deployment readiness: git status --porcelain returned ` M docs/agent-tool-contracts.md`, contradicting clean-release preflight and blocking release until resolved. Production readiness: schema/tool changes are structural Zod-backed contract hardening; no additional blocker found.

risks: docs/agent-tool-contracts.md dirty change appears contract-relevant and should probably be included, but reviewer did not commit or revert because that release packaging decision belongs to main agent.
required_main_agent_actions: Resolve dirty worktree before release: commit/include docs/agent-tool-contracts.md if intended, or revert it if not intended.
After resolving dirty docs, rerun git status --porcelain and confirm empty; rerun targeted docs/contract checks only if the doc content changes materially.
Then continue release gate; existing full verification evidence remains acceptable unless the dirty doc resolution changes source/test behavior.

proposal.approval_evidence: User agreed with transcript analysis and requested proposal creation: 'agree, create proposal'. Proposal includes problem, scope, success criteria, failure handling, proposed direction, and non-goals.

discovery.approval_evidence: Discovery findings recorded; user answered required scope/rollout/verification questions and approved AC with `approve`. agreement.md persisted and contract minted with 18 items.

No user-value tradeoff, unresolved CONFLICT, or contract-compromise risk.

items.text: Worker packets include first-class scope anchors: `TASK_SCOPE`, `IN_SCOPE`, `OUT_OF_SCOPE`, `DONE_WHEN`, `STOP_WHEN`, and `VERIFICATION`.
Engineer reports structurally capture `scope_drift` and `required_main_agent_actions`, not only prose `follow_ups`.
Legacy packets warn first during rollout; strict failure remains for identity anchors (`CHANGE`, `TASK`, `PHASE`, `ATTEMPT`, `WORKING DIRECTORY`).
Out-of-scope findings use “finish owned scope if safe, then report” by default; stop immediately only for contract/security/release blockers.
Verification commands are required when possible; workers may add extra checks.
Asset/schema/spec tests prove schema ↔ packet ↔ prompt alignment for identity, scope, done, stop, and verification anchors.
Scanner lanes remain non-persisted; only orchestrator-submitted scanner bundles use `adv_subagent_report_submit`.
`adv_subagent_report_submit` report tool typing is checked so object payloads are not string-serialized by MCP/schema drift.
Do not weaken `INVALID_REPORT` validation for required identity fields.
Do not infer missing `task_id`, `phase`, `attempt`, `change_id`, or `workdir_used` heuristically for persistence correctness.
Keep worker and scanner transport lanes distinct.
Use structural mechanisms first: Zod schemas, packet builders/anchors, asset tests, specs, and command/prompt checks.
Preserve backward compatibility for existing/legacy packets where possible by warning first for newly-added non-identity anchors.
Do not make sub-agents discover their own ADV task IDs from global state.
Do not rely on final-message fenced JSON as ADV worker report transport.
Do not make explore/scanner lanes call `adv_subagent_report_submit` directly.
Do not expand into unrelated sub-agent quality or model-routing refactors.

rows.evidence: Asset tests verify TASK_SCOPE, IN_SCOPE, OUT_OF_SCOPE, DONE_WHEN, STOP_WHEN, VERIFICATION in worker prompts/packets. Combined asset/schema test suite exitCode 0 (166 tests).
rows.notes: Covers adv-engineer, adv-reviewer, adv-researcher, adv-tron, and command packets.

rows.evidence: EngineerSubagentReportSchema now requires scope_drift and required_main_agent_actions; schema/tool tests pass (35 tests).
rows.notes: Engineer prompt payload and examples updated; examples parse through Zod.

rows.evidence: SUBAGENT_WARN_FIRST_PACKET_ANCHORS is separate from strict identity anchors; delegation-defaults uses warn_packet_anchors. Relevant schema/spec/matrix tests pass.
rows.notes: Identity anchors remain strict and schema-backed.

rows.evidence: Specs and prompts document finish owned scope if safe, then report; stop immediately for contract/security/release blockers. Asset tests assert prompt/spec coverage.
Worker packets include VERIFICATION sections with required_when_possible commands and optional_additional_checks. Full check, full tests, and build passed.
Schema/spec/asset alignment pinned by tests across subagent-reports, delegation-matrix, engineer/reviewer/optimized-handoff assets. Full vitest --maxWorkers=4 exitCode 0.
rows.notes: Includes identity, scope, done, stop, verification anchors.

rows.evidence: Specs and asset tests verify scanner packets are explore-only and do not ask scanners to call adv_subagent_report_submit; scanner bundles remain orchestrator-submitted only.
adv_subagent_report_submit args.report uses ScopedSubagentReportSchema; tests reject JSON-stringified payloads with INVALID_REPORT.
ScopedSubagentReportSchema remains strict; parseReport still returns INVALID_REPORT on malformed payloads. Tests cover missing/invalid fields and scope pairings.
No code infers missing task_id, phase, attempt, change_id, or workdir_used for persistence. Prompts forbid asking user or inferring identity values.
delegation-defaults and asset tests keep typed_persisted_worker vs non_persisted_scanner contracts separate.
Implementation uses Zod schemas, exported anchor constants, specs, matrix fields, and tests. pnpm run check, full tests, and build passed.
New non-identity anchors are warn-first constants/matrix fields; strict identity anchors remain separate and tested.
Agent prompts continue to forbid nested delegation; asset tests pin this. No nested spawning added.
Worker prompts copy identity from packet anchors and treat missing identity as packet defects; no worker discovers ADV task IDs from global state.
Prompts/specs require adv_subagent_report_submit tool-call transport and explicitly reject fenced JSON/sentinel transport.
Review/harden scanner packets stay explore-only and exclude adv_subagent_report_submit; asset/spec tests verify scanner isolation.
Touched scope was limited to sub-agent contracts: schemas/tool tests, specs, agent prompts, command packets, and fixtures. No unrelated refactors.

acceptanceCriteria: Worker packets include first-class scope anchors: `TASK_SCOPE`, `IN_SCOPE`, `OUT_OF_SCOPE`, `DONE_WHEN`, `STOP_WHEN`, and `VERIFICATION`.
Engineer reports structurally capture `scope_drift` and `required_main_agent_actions`, not only prose `follow_ups`.
Legacy packets warn first during rollout; strict failure remains for identity anchors (`CHANGE`, `TASK`, `PHASE`, `ATTEMPT`, `WORKING DIRECTORY`).
Out-of-scope findings use “finish owned scope if safe, then report” by default; stop immediately only for contract/security/release blockers.
Verification commands are required when possible; workers may add extra checks.
Asset/schema/spec tests prove schema ↔ packet ↔ prompt alignment for identity, scope, done, stop, and verification anchors.
Scanner lanes remain non-persisted; only orchestrator-submitted scanner bundles use `adv_subagent_report_submit`.
`adv_subagent_report_submit` report tool typing is checked so object payloads are not string-serialized by MCP/schema drift.

clarify_findings.message: Proposal describes behavior that could fail but doesn't mention error handling or rollback. What happens when things go wrong?
